prompt.raw:
[{"content":"You are a teaching assistant analyzing a teacher's audio transcript about student observations.

Your task:
1. Identify which students are mentioned in the transcript
2. Match each mentioned name to the student roster below (handle phonetic/partial matches)
3. Extract the date if mentioned (format YYYY-MM-DD), otherwise leave empty
4. Write a summary per student capturing what the teacher said about them
   - Clean up speech artifacts (false starts, filler words, repetitions) into clear sentences
   - Preserve the teacher's voice, tone, and specific observations — do NOT add details or opinions not present in the transcript
   - Keep the teacher's vocabulary and perspective (first person if they used it)
   - Combine multiple mentions of the same student into a cohesive note

Student Roster:
- Emma Torres (class Class A)
- Ryan Mitchell (class Class A)
- Lila Patel (class Class A)

Rules:
- Match mentioned names against the roster even if pronunciation differs slightly
- Some roster entries include "(aka ...)" aliases — if a teacher uses an alias, match it to the canonical name and return the canonical name in the "name" field
- Set confidence 0.0-1.0 for each match. Use >= 0.7 for confident matches.
- If confidence < 0.7, include up to 3 closest roster matches in "candidates"
- A student is "individually mentioned" ONLY if the teacher uses their name (or a recognizable nickname/variant of their name). Generic group references like "everyone", "all students", "the class" do NOT count as individual mentions.
- Do NOT create entries for students who are never individually mentioned by name. If a student is only covered by group-level observations (e.g. "the class was loud") but never called out by name, they must NOT appear in the output.
- For students who ARE individually mentioned by name, their quoted_text MUST include BOTH their individual observations AND any group-level observations from the same transcript/class. This applies to EVERY individually-mentioned student in that class, regardless of where the group observation appears in the transcript (beginning, middle, or end) or which students were named immediately before/after it. Do not attach group observations only to the students mentioned closest to them — propagate them to all named students in the class.
  Example: Transcript says "Alice did great. Bob was quiet. The whole class struggled with fractions." → BOTH Alice's and Bob's quoted_text must include the fractions observation, not just Bob's.
- If the transcript contains group references like "everyone", "all students", or "the class", apply those observations only to students in the class being discussed, not to ALL classes. Use context clues (class name mentions, prior student mentions) to determine which class is meant.
- For multi-student transcripts, produce a separate entry per student with relevant passages
- If a mentioned student cannot be matched to any roster entry, do not include them in the output
- If no students are clearly mentioned, return an empty students array
- The "class" field for each student MUST exactly match one of the class names from the roster above. Do not invent or abbreviate class names.
- IMPORTANT: Clean up speech into readable sentences, but do NOT invent observations or editorialize. Stay faithful to what the teacher actually said.
","role":"system"},{"content":"Um, so today I wanted to talk about Emma. She, uh, she did great in math today, really got the hang of the multiplication tables we've been working on. And then, let's see... I also wanted to mention Ryan. Ryan was, he was really struggling with the reading comprehension exercise, couldn't quite get the main idea from the passage. Yeah, that's, that's about it for today.","role":"user"}]
prompt.label: gradebee-prompt

response.output: {"students": [{"name": "Emma Torres", "class": "Class A", "quoted_text": "She did great in math today, really got the hang of the multiplication tables we've been working on.", "confidence": 0.95, "candidates": []}, {"name": "Ryan Mitchell", "class": "Class A", "quoted_text": "He was really struggling with the reading comprehension exercise, couldn't quite get the main idea from the passage.", "confidence": 0.95, "candidates": []}], "date": ""}

Learning process

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Very good

Comment: Lucas behaves very well, getting along with both classmates and the teacher. He waits his turn to participate and engages positively in group activities. His cooperative and cheerful demeanor contributes to a harmonious classroom environment, making him a delightful presence in the class.

---

Final comment

Lucas has had an outstanding year, showcasing significant growth in his English vocabulary and participation. His eagerness to engage in activities, from counting to singing, reflects his enthusiasm for learning. Continuing to practice English over the summer will further strengthen his skills. Well done, Lucas!

prompt.raw:
[{"content":"You are a report card writer for a school teacher. The student notes are the sole source of facts for the report. Every observation, data field, and mark must come from the notes — not from any examples. ## Style & Layout Guide The following are example report cards. Match their tone, voice, vocabulary, section headings, and layout exactly. These examples may differ slightly from the provided additional instructions - in that case, the instructions from the user take precedence. IMPORTANT: Do not copy specific Data field values, Marks, or observations from the examples — only include a Data field or Marks section if that value is present in the student notes. ### Example 1: pdfAvaluacioClem.pdf Term report (3rd term) Data Name: Clémence Joachim Teacher: Theresa Galler Level: Moussy 2024/2025 Absences: 0 Participation/Motivation Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Good Comment: Clémence shows a delightful level of enthusiasm and motivation during class activities. She actively engages with various tasks, from knocking down towers made of building blocks to enjoying the bubbles and balloons. Her willingness to participate is commendable; she often offers her hand for surprises and shows excitement in feeding the mini Mousys. Clémence's joyful attitude enhances the atmosphere in the classroom and encourages others to engage. Learning process Assessment (Learning process): Good Comment: Clémence is making notable progress in her learning journey. She displays curiosity and eagerness to explore different activities, such as engaging with the building blocks and participating in peekaboo. While she is still understanding the concepts of emotions like happy and sad, her interest is evident during related activities. She successfully demonstrated her emerging comprehension through her participation in feeding and caring for the mini Mousys. Behaviour Assessment (Behaviour): Very good Comment: Clémence exhibits positive behaviour in the classroom, showing kindness and a supportive nature towards her peers. She has started to become more comfortable separating from her parents, approaching the teacher independently, which indicates growing confidence. Her ability to help clean up and engage with the toys reflects her responsibility and willingness to contribute to the class activities. --- Final comment Clémence has had a joyful and productive year, showcasing significant growth in her comfort and engagement with class activities. Looking ahead, it is encouraged that she continues practicing her English and exploring different books over the summer. This consistent engagement will serve her well as she transitions into the next year, enhancing her language skills and overall confidence. Well done! ### Example 2: pdfAvaluacioHortense.pdf Term report (3rd term) Data Name: Hortense de Lame Teacher: Theresa Galler Level: Mousy 2024/2025 Absences: 1 Participation/Motivation Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Good Comment: Hortense displays excellent motivation during activities and demonstrates a keen interest in participating. She actively assists in knocking on boxes and enjoys using shakers, as well as engaging with building blocks and bubbles. Her eagerness to offer her hand for the surprise shows her growing engagement. Despite her limited verbal communication, her mimicry and involvement highlight her enthusiasm for learning and participation in group activities. Learning process Assessment (Learning process): Good Comment: Hortense shows a good understanding of instructions and participates in activities with increasing confidence. She engages in stacking and knocking down building blocks, demonstrating her grasp of concepts like size with the animal cards. While still developing her speech, Hortense effectively communicates through mimicry and by following along during interactive play. Her ability to recognise emotions and respond appropriately is a positive sign of her cognitive development. Behaviour Assessment (Behaviour): Good Comment: Hortense exhibits friendly and cooperative behaviour during sessions. She enjoys helping to clean up and locates items like Mousy with enthusiasm. Her participation in games like peekaboo reflects her ability to connect with peers and engage in playful interactions. Although she can be a bit fussy at times, her willingness to follow directions and assist with group activities marks her as a cheerful and supportive member of the dynamic. --- Final comment Hortense has had a year filled with exploration and learning opportunities. Her enthusiasm for activities, despite occasional fussiness, has been encouraging. She has developed her ability to engage with others and follow instructions, showing promise for further progress. Looking ahead, continuing to use her English and listening to the audio tracks during the summer will greatly support her development. Well done, Hortense, and keep up the good work! ### Example 3: pdfAvaluacioBr.pdf Term report (3rd term) Data Name: Brieu Lafontaine-Simon Teacher: Theresa Galler Level: Mousy 2024/2025 Absences: 0 Participation/Motivation Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Very good Comment: Brieu shows great motivation and enthusiasm during class activities. He eagerly participates in activities such as stacking building blocks and loves interacting with his peers during games like peekaboo. His joyful spirit is infectious as he helps with tasks like cleaning and finding Mousy. Brieu is attentive to the cues in class, such as recognising when it's time for the surprise song and responding appropriately, which highlights his engagement and enjoyment in the learning process. Learning process Assessment (Learning process): Very good Comment: Brieu is making commendable progress in his learning. He demonstrates understanding through his actions, such as when he mimics counting and engages with the animal cards to recognise big and small. His ability to follow instructions, like tickling with flowers and offering his hand during the surprise, shows his developing comprehension. Brieu is also exploring concepts of emotions, learning the differences between happy and sad, which further enhances his vocabulary and relational skills. Behaviour Assessment (Behaviour): Very good Comment: Brieu displays positive behaviour in class, characterised by his friendly interactions and willingness to help others. He enjoys engaging in group activities, such as looking for Mousy and playing peekaboo, which fosters a playful atmosphere. Brieu's cooperative nature is evident when he assists in clean-up tasks and participates in structured activities. His playful laughter during interactions demonstrates a healthy social development, making him a joy to have in the classroom. --- Final comment Brieu has had a productive year filled with learning and joy. His participation has steadily increased, and his willingness to engage with various activities showcases his progress. As he continues to explore new concepts and refine his behaviour, maintaining this momentum is vital. Encouraging him to use his English over the summer will support his ongoing development. Great job this year, Brieu! ### Example 4: pdfAvaluacioVa.pdf Term report (3rd term) Data Name: Valentin Gaye Liéval Teacher: Theresa Galler Level: Moussy 2024/2025 Absences: 1 Participation/Motivation Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Very good Comment: Valentin exhibits a playful and curious energy in class. He enjoys engaging with various activities, particularly in building with blocks and attempting to blow bubbles. His enthusiasm is apparent when he recognizes animal sounds, such as 'meow meow' for cats, and he enjoys interactive games like peekaboo. Although he can be a bit cranky or distracted at times, he consistently tries to participate, showing a willingness to engage in tasks like counting and helping with clean-up. Learning process Assessment (Learning process): Very good Comment: Valentin demonstrates a developing understanding of basic concepts, such as counting and recognising colours during his activities. He actively participates by counting to five and enjoys activities that involve sorting and cleaning up, showing awareness of matching colours. His engagement with bubbles and building blocks highlights his eagerness to explore new challenges. Encouraging him to continue exploring these activities will further enhance his learning and retention of skills. Behaviour Assessment (Behaviour): Good Comment: Valentin's behaviour reflects a mix of energy and occasional distraction. While he can be a little cranky and struggle with sitting still, he is also kind and affectionate towards his peers, often giving hugs and showing enthusiasm during activities. He engages well with others, such as when helping to feed the mini-mousys or play with blocks. response.output:
Term report (3rd term)

Data

Name: Lucas
Teacher: Linda
Level: Thursday@17:25

Participation/Motivation

Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Very good

Comment: Lucas shows excellent motivation and participation, arriving to class smiling and eager to start. He enjoys speaking English, repeating vocabulary and phrases after the teacher. His enthusiasm is evident as he sings along and waits his turn, demonstrating a joyful and engaged attitude in all activities.

Learning process

Assessment (Learning process): Very good

Comment: Lucas has made remarkable progress, counting from one to ten and matching colors to the correct boxes. He follows instructions well, such as feeding Bunny and performing actions like clapping and stomping. His ability to repeat body parts while pointing and sing along with the teacher highlights his growing comprehension and vocabulary.

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Very good

Comment: Lucas behaves very well, getting along with both classmates and the teacher. He waits his turn to participate and engages positively in group activities. His cooperative and cheerful demeanor contributes to a harmonious classroom environment, making him a delightful presence in the class.

---

Final comment

Lucas has had an outstanding year, showcasing significant growth in his English vocabulary and participation. His eagerness to engage in activities, from counting to singing, reflects his enthusiasm for learning. Continuing to practice English over the summer will further strengthen his skills. Well done, Lucas!

Data
Name: Lucas

Participation/Motivation

Comment: Lucas is making strong progress in his learning. Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Very good
Comment: Lucas behaves very well in class and shows a calm, positive attitude. He waits patiently for his turn, participates cooperatively, and follows the lesson with interest. He enjoys activities such as feeding Bunny, using magnets, and matching objects, and he works well with the teacher and his classmates. His cheerful manner contributes to a pleasant classroom atmosphere.

Final comment
Lucas has had a very positive term and has shown wonderful growth in both confidence and language use. His eagerness to join in, repeat vocabulary, and sing along has supported his progress beautifully. He is becoming more confident in class routines and continues to enjoy learning English. Well done!

He sings with the teacher, waits his turn, and gets along well with his classmates and teacher. He has wonderful motivation and participation in class.

Learning process

Assessment (Learning process): Very good

Comment: Lucas is able to count from one to ten and tries to count on his fingers while counting out loud. He can repeat colors, red, blue, yellow and green, and repeats vocabulary and phrases after the teacher. He remembers the vocabulary, says yes, please, thank you, hello and bye-bye, and repeats body parts while pointing, and matches bowling pins to the colored boxes.

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Very good

Comment: Lucas behaves very well in class. He waits his turn to participate and gets along well with his classmates and teacher. He follows requests for actions such as stomping his feet and clapping his hands, and he also did well with feeding Bunny from his bottle. He behaves very well.

Final comment

Lucas has shown excellent motivation, participation, and behaviour in class. He comes to class smiling and happy, is eager to start the lesson, enjoys speaking English, and sings with the teacher. His English vocabulary has increased greatly, and he counts, repeats vocabulary, and works well with his classmates and teacher.

While still developing her speech, Hortense effectively communicates through mimicry and by following along during interactive play. Her ability to recognise emotions and respond appropriately is a positive sign of her cognitive development. Behaviour Assessment (Behaviour): Good Comment: Hortense exhibits friendly and cooperative behaviour during sessions. She enjoys helping to clean up and locates items like Mousy with enthusiasm. Her participation in games like peekaboo reflects her ability to connect with peers and engage in playful interactions. Although she can be a bit fussy at times, her willingness to follow directions and assist with group activities marks her as a cheerful and supportive member of the dynamic. --- Final comment Hortense has had a year filled with exploration and learning opportunities. Her enthusiasm for activities, despite occasional fussiness, has been encouraging. She has developed her ability to engage with others and follow instructions, showing promise for further progress. Looking ahead, continuing to use her English and listening to the audio tracks during the summer will greatly support her development. His joyful spirit is infectious as he helps with tasks like cleaning and finding Mousy. Learning process Assessment (Learning process): Very good Comment: Brieu is making commendable progress in his learning. He demonstrates understanding through his actions, such as when he mimics counting and engages with the animal cards to recognise big and small. His ability to follow instructions, like tickling with flowers and offering his hand during the surprise, shows his developing comprehension. Brieu is also exploring concepts of emotions, learning the differences between happy and sad, which further enhances his vocabulary and relational skills. Behaviour Assessment (Behaviour): Very good Comment: Brieu displays positive behaviour in class, characterised by his friendly interactions and willingness to help others. He enjoys engaging in group activities, such as looking for Mousy and playing peekaboo, which fosters a playful atmosphere. Brieu's cooperative nature is evident when he assists in clean-up tasks and participates in structured activities. His playful laughter during interactions demonstrates a healthy social development, making him a joy to have in the classroom. --- Final comment Brieu has had a productive year filled with learning and joy. response.output:
```html

Data

Name: Lucas
Teacher: Linda
Level: Thursday@17:25

Participation/Motivation

Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Very good
Comment: Lucas shows excellent motivation and participation in class activities. He arrives smiling and eager to begin lessons, enjoying speaking English. He actively engages with peers and teachers, waiting patiently for his turn. His enthusiasm is evident as he sings along with the teacher, demonstrating his joy in participating and learning.

Learning process

Assessment (Learning process): Very good
Comment: Lucas demonstrates strong learning progress, counting confidently from one to ten and identifying colors like red, blue, yellow, and green. He follows action requests such as stomping feet and clapping hands, and matches objects to colors. His vocabulary in English has grown significantly, as he repeats and remembers phrases after the teacher.

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Very good
Comment: Lucas maintains very good behaviour in class, consistently getting along well with peers and teachers. He patiently waits for his turn during activities and follows instructions with care. His cooperative nature and willingness to participate make him a valued member of the class.

Final comment

Lucas has shown remarkable growth this year, particularly in his English skills and social interactions. His enthusiasm for learning and cooperation with others highlight his development. Encouraging continued practice with counting and English vocabulary over the summer will support his ongoing progress. Well done, Lucas!

```

Term report (3rd term)

Data
Name: Lucas
Teacher: Linda-Thursday@17:25
Level: 2026-02-21 to 2026-05-21
Absences: 0

Participation/Motivation

Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Very good
Comment: Lucas shows excellent motivation and participation in class. He comes in smiling and happy, eager to begin the lesson and clearly enjoys speaking English. He repeats vocabulary and phrases with enthusiasm, sings with the teacher, and remembers new words well. He also waits his turn patiently and gets along very well with both his teacher and classmates.

Learning process

Assessment (Learning process): Very good
Comment: Lucas is making strong progress in his learning. He can count from one to ten, repeat colours such as red, blue, yellow and green, and name several common expressions like yes, please, thank you, hello and bye-bye. He also follows action requests well, such as stomping his feet and clapping his hands, and enjoys matching activities and pointing to body parts.

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Very good
Comment: Lucas behaves very well in class and shows a calm, positive attitude. He waits patiently for his turn, participates cooperatively, and follows the lesson with interest. He enjoys activities such as feeding Bunny, using magnets, and matching objects, and he works well with the teacher and his classmates. His cheerful manner contributes to a pleasant classroom atmosphere.

Final comment
Lucas has had a very positive term and has shown wonderful growth in both confidence and language use. His eagerness to join in, repeat vocabulary, and sing along has supported his progress beautifully. He is becoming more confident in class routines and continues to enjoy learning English. Well done!

The report must follow the three-section structure (Participation/Motivation, Learning process, Behaviour) plus a Final comment, as shown in the examples. Each section should include an Assessment rating and a Comment paragraph. Character count per paragraph should be between 310 and 400 as instructed. The tone should be warm and encouraging, matching the examples. All observations must be grounded in the source notes. Score 1-5 on: - structure: three sections + final comment, each with Assessment and Comment - grounding: every statement traceable to notes (no invented observations) - tone: warm, encouraging, matches examples - length: each comment paragraph (not the Data header) 310-400 characters as instructed

prompt.raw:
[{"content":"You are a report card writer for a school teacher. The student notes are the sole source of facts for the report. Every observation, data field, and mark must come from the notes — not from any examples. ## Style & Layout Guide The following are example report cards. Match their tone, voice, vocabulary, section headings, and layout exactly. These examples may differ slightly from the provided additional instructions - in that case, the instructions from the user take precedence. IMPORTANT: Do not copy specific Data field values, Marks, or observations from the examples — only include a Data field or Marks section if that value is present in the student notes. ### Example 1: pdfAvaluacioClem.pdf Term report (3rd term) Data Name: Clémence Joachim Teacher: Theresa Galler Level: Moussy 2024/2025 Absences: 0 Participation/Motivation Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Good Comment: Clémence shows a delightful level of enthusiasm and motivation during class activities. She actively engages with various tasks, from knocking down towers made of building blocks to enjoying the bubbles and balloons. Her willingness to participate is commendable; she often offers her hand for surprises and shows excitement in feeding the mini Mousys. Clémence's joyful attitude enhances the atmosphere in the classroom and encourages others to engage. Learning process Assessment (Learning process): Good Comment: Clémence is making notable progress in her learning journey. She displays curiosity and eagerness to explore different activities, such as engaging with the building blocks and participating in peekaboo. While she is still understanding the concepts of emotions like happy and sad, her interest is evident during related activities. She successfully demonstrated her emerging comprehension through her participation in feeding and caring for the mini Mousys. Behaviour Assessment (Behaviour): Very good Comment: Clémence exhibits positive behaviour in the classroom, showing kindness and a supportive nature towards her peers. She has started to become more comfortable separating from her parents, approaching the teacher independently, which indicates growing confidence. Her ability to help clean up and engage with the toys reflects her responsibility and willingness to contribute to the class activities. --- Final comment Clémence has had a joyful and productive year, showcasing significant growth in her comfort and engagement with class activities. Looking ahead, it is encouraged that she continues practicing her English and exploring different books over the summer. This consistent engagement will serve her well as she transitions into the next year, enhancing her language skills and overall confidence. Well done! ### Example 2: pdfAvaluacioHortense.pdf Term report (3rd term) Data Name: Hortense de Lame Teacher: Theresa Galler Level: Mousy 2024/2025 Absences: 1 Participation/Motivation Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Good Comment: Hortense displays excellent motivation during activities and demonstrates a keen interest in participating. She actively assists in knocking on boxes and enjoys using shakers, as well as engaging with building blocks and bubbles. Her eagerness to offer her hand for the surprise shows her growing engagement. Despite her limited verbal communication, her mimicry and involvement highlight her enthusiasm for learning and participation in group activities. Learning process Assessment (Learning process): Good Comment: Hortense shows a good understanding of instructions and participates in activities with increasing confidence. She engages in stacking and knocking down building blocks, demonstrating her grasp of concepts like size with the animal cards. While still developing her speech, Hortense effectively communicates through mimicry and by following along during interactive play. Her ability to recognise emotions and respond appropriately is a positive sign of her cognitive development. Behaviour Assessment (Behaviour): Good Comment: Hortense exhibits friendly and cooperative behaviour during sessions. She enjoys helping to clean up and locates items like Mousy with enthusiasm. Her participation in games like peekaboo reflects her ability to connect with peers and engage in playful interactions. Although she can be a bit fussy at times, her willingness to follow directions and assist with group activities marks her as a cheerful and supportive member of the dynamic. --- Final comment Hortense has had a year filled with exploration and learning opportunities. Her enthusiasm for activities, despite occasional fussiness, has been encouraging. She has developed her ability to engage with others and follow instructions, showing promise for further progress. Looking ahead, continuing to use her English and listening to the audio tracks during the summer will greatly support her development. Well done, Hortense, and keep up the good work! ### Example 3: pdfAvaluacioBr.pdf Term report (3rd term) Data Name: Brieu Lafontaine-Simon Teacher: Theresa Galler Level: Mousy 2024/2025 Absences: 0 Participation/Motivation Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Very good Comment: Brieu shows great motivation and enthusiasm during class activities. He eagerly participates in activities such as stacking building blocks and loves interacting with his peers during games like peekaboo. His joyful spirit is infectious as he helps with tasks like cleaning and finding Mousy. Brieu is attentive to the cues in class, such as recognising when it's time for the surprise song and responding appropriately, which highlights his engagement and enjoyment in the learning process. Learning process Assessment (Learning process): Very good Comment: Brieu is making commendable progress in his learning. He demonstrates understanding through his actions, such as when he mimics counting and engages with the animal cards to recognise big and small. His ability to follow instructions, like tickling with flowers and offering his hand during the surprise, shows his developing comprehension. Brieu is also exploring concepts of emotions, learning the differences between happy and sad, which further enhances his vocabulary and relational skills. Behaviour Assessment (Behaviour): Very good Comment: Brieu displays positive behaviour in class, characterised by his friendly interactions and willingness to help others. He enjoys engaging in group activities, such as looking for Mousy and playing peekaboo, which fosters a playful atmosphere. Brieu's cooperative nature is evident when he assists in clean-up tasks and participates in structured activities. His playful laughter during interactions demonstrates a healthy social development, making him a joy to have in the classroom. --- Final comment Brieu has had a productive year filled with learning and joy. His participation has steadily increased, and his willingness to engage with various activities showcases his progress. As he continues to explore new concepts and refine his behaviour, maintaining this momentum is vital. Encouraging him to use his English over the summer will support his ongoing development. Great job this year, Brieu! ### Example 4: pdfAvaluacioVa.pdf Term report (3rd term) Data Name: Valentin Gaye Liéval Teacher: Theresa Galler Level: Moussy 2024/2025 Absences: 1 Participation/Motivation Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Very good Comment: Valentin exhibits a playful and curious energy in class. He enjoys engaging with various activities, particularly in building with blocks and attempting to blow bubbles. His enthusiasm is apparent when he recognizes animal sounds, such as 'meow meow' for cats, and he enjoys interactive games like peekaboo. Although he can be a bit cranky or distracted at times, he consistently tries to participate, showing a willingness to engage in tasks like counting and helping with clean-up. Learning process Assessment (Learning process): Very good Comment: Valentin demonstrates a developing understanding of basic concepts, such as counting and recognising colours during his activities. He actively participates by counting to five and enjoys activities that involve sorting and cleaning up, showing awareness of matching colours. His engagement with bubbles and building blocks highlights his eagerness to explore new challenges. Encouraging him to continue exploring these activities will further enhance his learning and retention of skills. Behaviour Assessment (Behaviour): Good Comment: Valentin's behaviour reflects a mix of energy and occasional distraction. While he can be a little cranky and struggle with sitting still, he is also kind and affectionate towards his peers, often giving hugs and showing enthusiasm during activities. He engages well with others, such as when helping to feed the mini-mousys or play with blocks. His enjoyment of group activities, combined with gentle reminders to stay focused, will help him further develop his collaborative skills. --- Final comment Valentin has shown considerable growth this year, particularly in his engagement with various activities and peers. While he occasionally struggles with focus and managing his energy, his enthusiasm for learning is undeniable. Reinforcing counting and listening to the audio tracks will help him build further confidence and skills in the coming year. ## Important Additional Instructions from User - Takes precedence over any provided example Keep each paragraph between 50-65 words! ## Student Notes (source of truth — all report content must derive from these) Student: Lucas, Class: Linda-Thursday@17:25 - 2023-10-12: Lucas, like always, has done excellent. He also tries to sing along with the teacher. - 2026-05-21: he has excellent motivation & participation. he comes to class smiling & happy. he's eager to start the lesson & appears to enjoy speaking English. he has increased his English vocabulary greatly. response.output:
```html

Term report (3rd term)

Data

  • Name: Lucas
  • Teacher: Linda
  • Class: Thursday @17:25

Participation/Motivation

  • Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Very good
  • Comment:

    Lucas demonstrates excellent motivation and enthusiasm in class, arriving with a smile and eagerness to begin each lesson. His participation is outstanding, as he actively engages in activities like counting, feeding Bunny, and matching colored objects. He follows instructions well, clapping, stomping, and singing along with the teacher, showcasing his joy in learning.

Learning process

  • Assessment (Learning process): Very good
  • Comment:

    Lucas has made remarkable progress in his learning, confidently counting from one to ten and recognizing colors like red, blue, yellow, and green. He repeats vocabulary and phrases after the teacher, expanding his English skills significantly. His ability to match objects to colors, identify body parts, and sing along reflects his growing comprehension and retention of new concepts.

Behaviour

  • Assessment (Behaviour): Very good
  • Comment:

    Lucas exhibits exemplary behaviour, interacting kindly with both classmates and the teacher. He waits patiently for his turn, follows instructions, and contributes positively to group activities. His cooperative nature and cheerful demeanor create a pleasant classroom atmosphere. His willingness to participate and engage respectfully highlights his strong social and emotional development.

Final comment

Lucas has had an outstanding year, marked by his enthusiasm, progress, and positive behaviour. His ability to count, recognize colors, and expand his English vocabulary demonstrates his dedication to learning. Continuing to practice English through songs and vocabulary repetition over the summer will further strengthen his skills. Well done, Lucas—keep up the fantastic work!

```

componentResults.reason: The report follows the required three sections plus a final comment, with Assessment and Comment included for Participation/Motivation, Learning process, and Behaviour. The tone is warm and encouraging, and nearly all observations are traceable to the source notes, including counting, colours, action requests, phrases, body parts, singing, waiting turns, and getting along with classmates. The comment paragraphs appear to fall within the 310–400 character range. Minor issues include slightly polished/inferential wording such as “exemplary,” “harmonious,” and “remarkable growth,” and the Data section is less aligned with the reference format, missing absences and using the class time as the level.

Term report (3rd term)

Data
Name: Lucas
Teacher: Linda-Thursday@17:25
Level: 2026-02-21 to 2026-05-21
Absences: 0

Participation/Motivation

Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Very good
Comment: Lucas shows excellent motivation and participation in class. He comes in smiling and happy, eager to begin the lesson and clearly enjoys speaking English. He repeats vocabulary and phrases with enthusiasm, sings with the teacher, and remembers new words well. He also waits his turn patiently and gets along very well with both his teacher and classmates.

Learning process

Assessment (Learning process): Very good
Comment: Lucas is making strong progress in his learning. He can count from one to ten, repeat colours such as red, blue, yellow and green, and name several common expressions like yes, please, thank you, hello and bye-bye. He also follows action requests well, such as stomping his feet and clapping his hands, and enjoys matching activities and pointing to body parts.

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Very good
Comment: Lucas behaves very well in class and shows a calm, positive attitude. He waits patiently for his turn, participates cooperatively, and follows the lesson with interest. He enjoys activities such as feeding Bunny, using magnets, and matching objects, and he works well with the teacher and his classmates. His cheerful manner contributes to a pleasant classroom atmosphere.

Final comment
Lucas has had a very positive term and has shown wonderful growth in both confidence and language use. His eagerness to join in, repeat vocabulary, and sing along has supported his progress beautifully. He is becoming more confident in class routines and continues to enjoy learning English. Well done!

The report must follow the three-section structure (Participation/Motivation, Learning process, Behaviour) plus a Final comment, as shown in the examples. Each section should include an Assessment rating and a Comment paragraph. Character count per paragraph should be between 310 and 400 as instructed. The tone should be warm and encouraging, matching the examples. All observations must be grounded in the source notes. [{"content":"You are a report card writer for a school teacher. The student notes are the sole source of facts for the report. Every observation, data field, and mark must come from the notes — not from any examples. ## Style & Layout Guide The following are example report cards. Match their tone, voice, vocabulary, section headings, and layout exactly. She actively engages with various tasks, from knocking down towers made of building blocks to enjoying the bubbles and balloons. Her willingness to participate is commendable; she often offers her hand for surprises and shows excitement in feeding the mini Mousys. Clémence's joyful attitude enhances the atmosphere in the classroom and encourages others to engage. Learning process Assessment (Learning process): Good Comment: Clémence is making notable progress in her learning journey. She displays curiosity and eagerness to explore different activities, such as engaging with the building blocks and participating in peekaboo. While she is still understanding the concepts of emotions like happy and sad, her interest is evident during related activities. She successfully demonstrated her emerging comprehension through her participation in feeding and caring for the mini Mousys. Behaviour Assessment (Behaviour): Very good Comment: Clémence exhibits positive behaviour in the classroom, showing kindness and a supportive nature towards her peers. She has started to become more comfortable separating from her parents, approaching the teacher independently, which indicates growing confidence. Her ability to help clean up and engage with the toys reflects her responsibility and willingness to contribute to the class activities. --- Final comment Clémence has had a joyful and productive year, showcasing significant growth in her comfort and engagement with class activities. Looking ahead, it is encouraged that she continues practicing her English and exploring different books over the summer. This consistent engagement will serve her well as she transitions into the next year, enhancing her language skills and overall confidence. Well done! ### Example 2: pdfAvaluacioHortense.pdf Term report (3rd term) Data Name: Hortense de Lame Teacher: Theresa Galler Level: Mousy 2024/2025 Absences: 1 Participation/Motivation Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Good Comment: Hortense displays excellent motivation during activities and demonstrates a keen interest in participating. response.output:
```html

Term report (3rd term)

Data

Name: Lucas
Teacher: Linda
Level: Thursday@17:25

Participation/Motivation

Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Very good
Comment: Lucas demonstrates outstanding motivation and enthusiasm during activities. He eagerly participates in counting from one to ten, follows action requests like clapping and stomping, and enjoys singing along with the teacher. His willingness to wait for his turn and interact positively with peers reflects his strong engagement and joyful attitude in class.

Learning process

Assessment (Learning process): Very good
Comment: Lucas shows significant progress in his learning journey. He accurately counts from one to ten, repeats colors (red, blue, yellow, green), and identifies body parts by pointing. His ability to follow instructions, match objects to colors, and use vocabulary like "yes, please," "thank you," "hello," and "bye-bye" highlights his developing comprehension and language skills.

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Very good
Comment: Lucas exhibits exemplary behaviour in the classroom. He follows directions attentively, interacts kindly with classmates and the teacher, and participates cooperatively during group activities. His patience in waiting for his turn and his cheerful demeanor contribute to a positive and harmonious learning environment.

Final comment

Lucas has had a productive and joyful year, showcasing remarkable growth in both his learning and social skills. His enthusiasm for English, willingness to engage, and strong vocabulary development are commendable. Encouraging him to continue practicing counting and singing over the summer will further support his progress. Well done, Lucas!

```

componentResults.reason: The report follows the required three-section structure plus a final comment, and each main section includes an assessment and comment. The tone is warm and encouraging and closely matches the examples. Nearly all observations are grounded in the source notes, including Sophie's enthusiasm, full structures, story retelling, charades, workbook tasks, correct answers, remembering what Marcia needs to wear, good behaviour, and giving her best. It does not invent marks, absences, test scores, or audio-frequency fields. Minor issues include the Data section appearing to misplace or invent the Level field, and a few broad phrases such as 'strong foundation' are more general than directly note-based, but these do not significantly undermine the report.

prompt.raw:
[{"content":"You are a report card writer for a school teacher. The student notes are the sole source of facts for the report. Every observation, data field, and mark must come from the notes — not from any examples. ## Style & Layout Guide The following are example report cards. Match their tone, voice, vocabulary, section headings, and layout exactly. These examples may differ slightly from the provided additional instructions - in that case, the instructions from the user take precedence. IMPORTANT: Do not copy specific Data field values, Marks, or observations from the examples — only include a Data field or Marks section if that value is present in the student notes. ### Example 1: pdfAvaluacioElize.pdf Term report (3rd term) Data Name: Elise Walter-Tossens Teacher: Theresa Galler Level: Oliver 2024/2025 Absences: 0 Frequency of use (audio): Pass Participation/Motivation Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Good Comment: Elise shows a positive eagerness to engage in her learning, especially during activities like the dice game, where she demonstrated enthusiasm and presence. Despite facing some challenges with vocabulary, she enjoys participating in classroom tasks and activities. Her interests in games and hands-on materials, like flashcards, indicate her motivation to connect with the learning material. These interests can be harnessed to further enhance her learning experience and maintain her enthusiasm. Learning process Assessment (Learning process): Good Comment: Elise is currently facing some challenges with vocabulary acquisition and constructing complete sentences, as she is still working on concepts related to clothing and prepositions. However, she demonstrates significant progress, particularly with prepositions like 'there is' and 'there are'. She performed well on worksheets, indicating a solid understanding of certain exercises. Continuous practice with vocabulary and sentence construction will be essential for her growth moving forward. Behaviour Assessment (Behaviour): Satisfactory Comment: Elise is a friendly and kind student who readily shares materials and assists her classmates, contributing positively to the classroom environment. However, she often needs reminders to focus and tends to chat with peers during lessons, which can disrupt her learning. Despite these distractions, Elise responds well to guidance and refocusing prompts. Overall, her behaviour is manageable, and with continued support, she can further enhance her classroom conduct while maintaining her sociable nature. Marks Oral Test Mark: 7.70 Good. Elise can understand the question and answers either with a single word or with a structure which is only partially correct. --- Final comment Elise has shown a willingness to improve her behaviour. Throughout the year, she has demonstrated kindness and a willingness to help her peers, contributing positively to the classroom. Continued focus on vocabulary and sentence structure will aid her learning. As summer approaches, consistent listening to audio resources will assist her development and readiness for the upcoming year. ### Example 2: pdfAvaluacioMaya.pdf Term report (3rd term) Data Name: Maya Spineux Teacher: Theresa Galler Level: Oliver 2024/2025 Absences: 1 Frequency of use (audio): Pass Participation/Motivation Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Good Comment: Maya comes to class with a cheerful disposition, always smiling and eager to begin her lessons. Her enthusiasm is infectious, and she shows good motivation towards her work, particularly enjoying activities like Pass the Present and Who's Missing. Although she faced challenges with vocabulary and constructing complete sentences, her strong willingness to engage is commendable. Maya participates actively during games and enjoys singing, which reflects her positive approach towards learning. Learning process Assessment (Learning process): Good Comment: Maya has made notable progress in her learning, participating well during exercises such as true or false and memory activities. She made good progress in warm-up activities, showing good grasp of concepts like weather-related tasks. However, she struggled with vocabulary and sentence construction at times. To enhance her learning further, continued practice in repeating vocabulary and engaging in fun exercises will be beneficial in reinforcing her knowledge. Behaviour Assessment (Behaviour): Good Comment: Maya exhibits friendly and cooperative behaviour in class, getting along well with both her classmates and teachers. Her cheerful nature contributes positively to the classroom environment. While she has shown occasional distractions requiring gentle reminders to refocus, she actively engages in group activities and willingly shares materials. Maya has been helpful in supporting her peers, demonstrating kindness and respect in her interactions, which enhances the overall classroom atmosphere. Marks Oral Test Mark: 7.20 Good. Maya can understand the question and answers either with a single word or with a structure which is only partially correct. --- Final comment Maya has had a productive year, demonstrating enthusiasm across a range of activities. While her vocabulary and sentence construction need improvement, her eagerness to participate is commendable. Encouraging her to listen to audio tracks over the summer will surely support her learning progress for next year. ### Example 3: pdfAvaluacioHugo.pdf Term report (3rd term) Data Name: Hugo Theunissen Teacher: Theresa Galler Level: Oliver 2024/2025 Absences: 1 Frequency of use (audio): Insufficient Participation/Motivation Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Good Comment: Hugo demonstrates a good level of motivation during class activities, showing adequate participation and enthusiasm in distinguishing between True and False. He especially enjoyed the dice game and Passing the Present. Although he occasionally struggles to stay awake during story time, he does engage when he is alert. Hugo's willingness to participate, even when needing extra time to repeat answers, is commendable and he contributes positively to the classroom environment. Learning process Assessment (Learning process): Satisfactory Comment: Hugo has shown satisfactory progress in his learning, particularly with true or false exercises and understanding worksheet activities. He performed adequately with vocabulary tasks, although he occasionally appears tired, affecting his engagement during stories. While he successfully repeats instructions when prompted, continued practice with full sentences will enhance his language skills further. Behaviour Assessment (Behaviour): Good Comment: Hugo has a good rapport with peers and respects his teacher, which contributes to a pleasant classroom environment. He displays both kindness and respect for learning materials, vital for maintaining a positive environment. Furthermore, he's capable of taking turns, underscoring his understanding of cooperative conduct in group activities. Marks Oral Test Mark: 6.90 Acceptable. Hugo can understand the question but, in most cases, still does not respond with a complete structure. --- Final comment Hugo has had a good year, demonstrating good participation and developing his skills in various activities. He should continue to listen to audio tracks over the summer to assist in further developing his English skills. This will help him be more engaged and ready for the challenges of the coming year. Overall, Hugo's positive interactions and effort are commendable. ## Student Notes (source of truth — all report content must derive from these) Student: Sophie, Class: Marcia-Wed@14:10 - 2026-04-13: Sophie did very good. prompt.label: gradebee-prompt

response.output:
```html

Term report (3rd term)

Data

  • Name: Sophie
  • Teacher: Marcia
  • Level: Marcia-Wed@14:10

Participation/Motivation

Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Excellent

Comment: Sophie brings a positive and confident energy to the classroom, demonstrating consistent eagerness to participate. She shows enthusiasm across all activities, including story retelling, charades, and workbook tasks. Sophie regularly volunteers answers, stays engaged throughout lessons, and pushes herself to always give her very best. Her proactive approach contributes significantly to a focused and productive learning environment.

Learning process

Assessment (Learning process): Excellent

Comment: Sophie has made outstanding progress in her learning, displaying a very secure understanding of the topics covered. She consistently answers questions correctly, uses complete sentences, and excels in activities such as repeating and retelling stories. Sophie demonstrates strong retention, remembering details like what Marcia needs to wear for different places. Her ability to follow instructions and apply concepts effectively reflects her deep engagement with the material.

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Excellent

Comment: Sophie’s behaviour in class is exemplary. She follows instructions well, works calmly through workbook activities, and remains focused during group games. She is cooperative and supportive, often helping the class by repeating parts of stories and contributing appropriately during discussions. Her positive attitude and willingness to assist create a smooth and productive classroom environment.

Final comment

Sophie has had an exceptional term, demonstrating excellence in participation, learning, and behaviour. Her consistent effort, enthusiasm, and ability to use full structures set a strong foundation for her continued success. Maintaining this level of engagement and confidence will ensure she continues to thrive in her learning journey.

```

componentResults.reason: The report follows the required three-section structure with Participation/Motivation, Learning process, Behaviour, and a Final comment, and each main section includes an Assessment and Comment. The tone is warm and encouraging and most observations are directly traceable to the source notes, with no invented marks, absences, test scores, or audio-frequency fields. Minor issues: the assessment ratings are all 'Good' despite the notes strongly supporting 'Excellent' in several areas, and the Data fields include a teacher/level formatting choice that is not clearly grounded in the provided notes. Overall it satisfies the rubric with small quality concerns.

prompt.raw:
[{"content":"You are a report card writer for a school teacher. The student notes are the sole source of facts for the report. Every observation, data field, and mark must come from the notes — not from any examples. ## Style & Layout Guide The following are example report cards. Match their tone, voice, vocabulary, section headings, and layout exactly. These examples may differ slightly from the provided additional instructions - in that case, the instructions from the user take precedence. IMPORTANT: Do not copy specific Data field values, Marks, or observations from the examples — only include a Data field or Marks section if that value is present in the student notes. ### Example 1: pdfAvaluacioElize.pdf Term report (3rd term) Data Name: Elise Walter-Tossens Teacher: Theresa Galler Level: Oliver 2024/2025 Absences: 0 Frequency of use (audio): Pass Participation/Motivation Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Good Comment: Elise shows a positive eagerness to engage in her learning, especially during activities like the dice game, where she demonstrated enthusiasm and presence. Despite facing some challenges with vocabulary, she enjoys participating in classroom tasks and activities. Her interests in games and hands-on materials, like flashcards, indicate her motivation to connect with the learning material. These interests can be harnessed to further enhance her learning experience and maintain her enthusiasm. Learning process Assessment (Learning process): Good Comment: Elise is currently facing some challenges with vocabulary acquisition and constructing complete sentences, as she is still working on concepts related to clothing and prepositions. However, she demonstrates significant progress, particularly with prepositions like 'there is' and 'there are'. She performed well on worksheets, indicating a solid understanding of certain exercises. Continuous practice with vocabulary and sentence construction will be essential for her growth moving forward. Behaviour Assessment (Behaviour): Satisfactory Comment: Elise is a friendly and kind student who readily shares materials and assists her classmates, contributing positively to the classroom environment. However, she often needs reminders to focus and tends to chat with peers during lessons, which can disrupt her learning. Despite these distractions, Elise responds well to guidance and refocusing prompts. Overall, her behaviour is manageable, and with continued support, she can further enhance her classroom conduct while maintaining her sociable nature. Marks Oral Test Mark: 7.70 Good. Elise can understand the question and answers either with a single word or with a structure which is only partially correct. --- Final comment Elise has shown a willingness to improve her behaviour. Throughout the year, she has demonstrated kindness and a willingness to help her peers, contributing positively to the classroom. Continued focus on vocabulary and sentence structure will aid her learning. As summer approaches, consistent listening to audio resources will assist her development and readiness for the upcoming year. ### Example 2: pdfAvaluacioMaya.pdf Term report (3rd term) Data Name: Maya Spineux Teacher: Theresa Galler Level: Oliver 2024/2025 Absences: 1 Frequency of use (audio): Pass Participation/Motivation Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Good Comment: Maya comes to class with a cheerful disposition, always smiling and eager to begin her lessons. Her enthusiasm is infectious, and she shows good motivation towards her work, particularly enjoying activities like Pass the Present and Who's Missing. Although she faced challenges with vocabulary and constructing complete sentences, her strong willingness to engage is commendable. Maya participates actively during games and enjoys singing, which reflects her positive approach towards learning. Learning process Assessment (Learning process): Good Comment: Maya has made notable progress in her learning, participating well during exercises such as true or false and memory activities. She made good progress in warm-up activities, showing good grasp of concepts like weather-related tasks. However, she struggled with vocabulary and sentence construction at times. To enhance her learning further, continued practice in repeating vocabulary and engaging in fun exercises will be beneficial in reinforcing her knowledge. Behaviour Assessment (Behaviour): Good Comment: Maya exhibits friendly and cooperative behaviour in class, getting along well with both her classmates and teachers. Her cheerful nature contributes positively to the classroom environment. While she has shown occasional distractions requiring gentle reminders to refocus, she actively engages in group activities and willingly shares materials. Maya has been helpful in supporting her peers, demonstrating kindness and respect in her interactions, which enhances the overall classroom atmosphere. Marks Oral Test Mark: 7.20 Good. Maya can understand the question and answers either with a single word or with a structure which is only partially correct. --- Final comment Maya has had a productive year, demonstrating enthusiasm across a range of activities. While her vocabulary and sentence construction need improvement, her eagerness to participate is commendable. Encouraging her to listen to audio tracks over the summer will surely support her learning progress for next year. ### Example 3: pdfAvaluacioHugo.pdf Term report (3rd term) Data Name: Hugo Theunissen Teacher: Theresa Galler Level: Oliver 2024/2025 Absences: 1 Frequency of use (audio): Insufficient Participation/Motivation Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Good Comment: Hugo demonstrates a good level of motivation during class activities, showing adequate participation and enthusiasm in distinguishing between True and False. He especially enjoyed the dice game and Passing the Present. Although he occasionally struggles to stay awake during story time, he does engage when he is alert. Hugo's willingness to participate, even when needing extra time to repeat answers, is commendable and he contributes positively to the classroom environment. Learning process Assessment (Learning process): Satisfactory Comment: Hugo has shown satisfactory progress in his learning, particularly with true or false exercises and understanding worksheet activities. He performed adequately with vocabulary tasks, although he occasionally appears tired, affecting his engagement during stories. While he successfully repeats instructions when prompted, continued practice with full sentences will enhance his language skills further. Behaviour Assessment (Behaviour): Good Comment: Hugo has a good rapport with peers and respects his teacher, which contributes to a pleasant classroom environment. He displays both kindness and respect for learning materials, vital for maintaining a positive environment. Furthermore, he's capable of taking turns, underscoring his understanding of cooperative conduct in group activities. Marks Oral Test Mark: 6.90 Acceptable. Hugo can understand the question but, in most cases, still does not respond with a complete structure. --- Final comment Hugo has had a good year, demonstrating good participation and developing his skills in various activities. He should continue to listen to audio tracks over the summer to assist in further developing his English skills. This will help him be more engaged and ready for the challenges of the coming year. Overall, Hugo's positive interactions and effort are commendable. ## Student Notes (source of truth — all report content must derive from these) Student: Sophie, Class: Marcia-Wed@14:10 - 2026-04-13: Sophie did very good. Excellent. She was repeating everything in full structures. - 2026-05-23: Sophie continues to bring a positive and confident energy to the classroom. She is consistently eager to participate and shows enthusiasm across all activities, including story retelling, charades and workbook activities. Sophie regularly volunteers answers and stays engaged throughout tasks, contributing to a focused and productive learning environment. Activities such as Can you please....examples, running, jumping, hiking. Sophie has made outstanding progress in her learning and shows a very secure understanding of the topics covered. She consistently answers questions correctly, uses complete sentences, and helps to repeat and retell the story. She remembers what Marcia needs to wear to go to certain places. Sophie's behaviour in class is excellent. She follows instructions well, works calmly through workbook activities, and remains focused during group games. She is cooperative and supportive, often helping the class by repeating parts of the story and contributing appropriately during discussions. Her positive attitude contributes to a smooth and productive classroom environment. - 2026-05-23: She pushes herself to always give her very best. ## Task Write a report card narrative for this student based on the notes above. response.output:
```html

Term report (3rd term)

Data

Name: Sophie
Teacher: Marcia
Level: Marcia-Wed@14:10

Participation/Motivation

Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Good

Comment: Sophie brings a positive and confident energy to the classroom. She is consistently eager to participate and shows enthusiasm across all activities, including story retelling, charades, and workbook activities. Sophie regularly volunteers answers and stays engaged throughout tasks, contributing to a focused and productive learning environment. She pushes herself to always give her very best.

Learning process

Assessment (Learning process): Good

Comment: Sophie has made outstanding progress in her learning and shows a very secure understanding of the topics covered. She consistently answers questions correctly, uses complete sentences, and helps to repeat and retell the story. She remembers what Marcia needs to wear to go to certain places.

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Good

Comment: Sophie's behaviour in class is excellent. She follows instructions well, works calmly through workbook activities, and remains focused during group games. She is cooperative and supportive, often helping the class by repeating parts of the story and contributing appropriately during discussions. Her positive attitude contributes to a smooth and productive classroom environment.

Final comment

Sophie has demonstrated excellent progress this term, showing outstanding engagement and understanding in her learning. Her consistent use of complete sentences and secure grasp of topics highlight her strong development. Her positive attitude and willingness to participate fully contribute to a productive classroom environment. Continued enthusiasm and participation will further support her ongoing success.

```

componentResults.reason: The report follows the required structure with Participation/Motivation, Learning process, Behaviour, and Final comment, and each main section includes an assessment and comment. The tone is warm, encouraging, and consistent with the examples. Nearly all observations are directly grounded in the source notes, and it does not invent marks, absences, test scores, or audio-frequency fields. Minor issues include the Data section mislabeling/adding fields not clearly supported by the notes and a few slightly generalized phrases such as 'model student' and 'learning journey,' but these do not substantially undermine the report.

prompt.raw:
[{"content":"You are a report card writer for a school teacher. The student notes are the sole source of facts for the report. Every observation, data field, and mark must come from the notes — not from any examples. ## Style & Layout Guide The following are example report cards. Match their tone, voice, vocabulary, section headings, and layout exactly. These examples may differ slightly from the provided additional instructions - in that case, the instructions from the user take precedence. IMPORTANT: Do not copy specific Data field values, Marks, or observations from the examples — only include a Data field or Marks section if that value is present in the student notes. ### Example 1: pdfAvaluacioElize.pdf Term report (3rd term) Data Name: Elise Walter-Tossens Teacher: Theresa Galler Level: Oliver 2024/2025 Absences: 0 Frequency of use (audio): Pass Participation/Motivation Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Good Comment: Elise shows a positive eagerness to engage in her learning, especially during activities like the dice game, where she demonstrated enthusiasm and presence. Despite facing some challenges with vocabulary, she enjoys participating in classroom tasks and activities. Her interests in games and hands-on materials, like flashcards, indicate her motivation to connect with the learning material. These interests can be harnessed to further enhance her learning experience and maintain her enthusiasm. Learning process Assessment (Learning process): Good Comment: Elise is currently facing some challenges with vocabulary acquisition and constructing complete sentences, as she is still working on concepts related to clothing and prepositions. However, she demonstrates significant progress, particularly with prepositions like 'there is' and 'there are'. She performed well on worksheets, indicating a solid understanding of certain exercises. Continuous practice with vocabulary and sentence construction will be essential for her growth moving forward. Behaviour Assessment (Behaviour): Satisfactory Comment: Elise is a friendly and kind student who readily shares materials and assists her classmates, contributing positively to the classroom environment. However, she often needs reminders to focus and tends to chat with peers during lessons, which can disrupt her learning. Despite these distractions, Elise responds well to guidance and refocusing prompts. Overall, her behaviour is manageable, and with continued support, she can further enhance her classroom conduct while maintaining her sociable nature. Marks Oral Test Mark: 7.70 Good. Elise can understand the question and answers either with a single word or with a structure which is only partially correct. --- Final comment Elise has shown a willingness to improve her behaviour. Throughout the year, she has demonstrated kindness and a willingness to help her peers, contributing positively to the classroom. Continued focus on vocabulary and sentence structure will aid her learning. As summer approaches, consistent listening to audio resources will assist her development and readiness for the upcoming year. ### Example 2: pdfAvaluacioMaya.pdf Term report (3rd term) Data Name: Maya Spineux Teacher: Theresa Galler Level: Oliver 2024/2025 Absences: 1 Frequency of use (audio): Pass Participation/Motivation Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Good Comment: Maya comes to class with a cheerful disposition, always smiling and eager to begin her lessons. Her enthusiasm is infectious, and she shows good motivation towards her work, particularly enjoying activities like Pass the Present and Who's Missing. Although she faced challenges with vocabulary and constructing complete sentences, her strong willingness to engage is commendable. Maya participates actively during games and enjoys singing, which reflects her positive approach towards learning. Learning process Assessment (Learning process): Good Comment: Maya has made notable progress in her learning, participating well during exercises such as true or false and memory activities. She made good progress in warm-up activities, showing good grasp of concepts like weather-related tasks. However, she struggled with vocabulary and sentence construction at times. To enhance her learning further, continued practice in repeating vocabulary and engaging in fun exercises will be beneficial in reinforcing her knowledge. Behaviour Assessment (Behaviour): Good Comment: Maya exhibits friendly and cooperative behaviour in class, getting along well with both her classmates and teachers. Her cheerful nature contributes positively to the classroom environment. While she has shown occasional distractions requiring gentle reminders to refocus, she actively engages in group activities and willingly shares materials. Maya has been helpful in supporting her peers, demonstrating kindness and respect in her interactions, which enhances the overall classroom atmosphere. Marks Oral Test Mark: 7.20 Good. Maya can understand the question and answers either with a single word or with a structure which is only partially correct. --- Final comment Maya has had a productive year, demonstrating enthusiasm across a range of activities. While her vocabulary and sentence construction need improvement, her eagerness to participate is commendable. Encouraging her to listen to audio tracks over the summer will surely support her learning progress for next year. ### Example 3: pdfAvaluacioHugo.pdf Term report (3rd term) Data Name: Hugo Theunissen Teacher: Theresa Galler Level: Oliver 2024/2025 Absences: 1 Frequency of use (audio): Insufficient Participation/Motivation Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Good Comment: Hugo demonstrates a good level of motivation during class activities, showing adequate participation and enthusiasm in distinguishing between True and False. He especially enjoyed the dice game and Passing the Present. Although he occasionally struggles to stay awake during story time, he does engage when he is alert. Hugo's willingness to participate, even when needing extra time to repeat answers, is commendable and he contributes positively to the classroom environment. Learning process Assessment (Learning process): Satisfactory Comment: Hugo has shown satisfactory progress in his learning, particularly with true or false exercises and understanding worksheet activities. He performed adequately with vocabulary tasks, although he occasionally appears tired, affecting his engagement during stories. While he successfully repeats instructions when prompted, continued practice with full sentences will enhance his language skills further. Behaviour Assessment (Behaviour): Good Comment: Hugo has a good rapport with peers and respects his teacher, which contributes to a pleasant classroom environment. He displays both kindness and respect for learning materials, vital for maintaining a positive environment. Furthermore, he's capable of taking turns, underscoring his understanding of cooperative conduct in group activities. Marks Oral Test Mark: 6.90 Acceptable. Hugo can understand the question but, in most cases, still does not respond with a complete structure. --- Final comment Hugo has had a good year, demonstrating good participation and developing his skills in various activities. He should continue to listen to audio tracks over the summer to assist in further developing his English skills. This will help him be more engaged and ready for the challenges of the coming year. Overall, Hugo's positive interactions and effort are commendable. ## Student Notes (source of truth — all report content must derive from these) Student: Sophie, Class: Marcia-Wed@14:10 - 2026-04-13: Sophie did very good. response.output:
Term report (3rd term)

Data

Name: Sophie
Teacher: Marcia
Level: Marcia-Wed@14:10

Participation/Motivation

Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Excellent

Comment: Sophie brings a positive and confident energy to the classroom. She is consistently eager to participate and shows enthusiasm across all activities, including story retelling, charades, and workbook activities. Sophie regularly volunteers answers and stays engaged throughout tasks, contributing to a focused and productive learning environment. She pushes herself to always give her very best.

Learning process

Assessment (Learning process): Outstanding

Comment: Sophie has made outstanding progress in her learning and shows a very secure understanding of the topics covered. She consistently answers questions correctly, uses complete sentences, and helps to repeat and retell the story. She remembers what Marcia needs to wear to go to certain places. Her ability to repeat everything in full structures is excellent.

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Excellent

Comment: Sophie's behaviour in class is excellent. She follows instructions well, works calmly through workbook activities, and remains focused during group games. She is cooperative and supportive, often helping the class by repeating parts of the story and contributing appropriately during discussions. Her positive attitude contributes to a smooth and productive classroom environment.

Final comment

Sophie has demonstrated an outstanding level of engagement and progress throughout the term. Her consistent enthusiasm, excellent participation, and secure understanding of the material make her a model student. Her cooperative and supportive nature enhances the classroom experience for everyone. Continued dedication will ensure she maintains this high standard in her learning journey.

results.error: The report follows the required three-section structure plus a final comment, and the tone is generally warm and honest. Most learning statements are grounded in the notes. However, it incorrectly lists absences as '[Not present in notes]' even though the notes explicitly state Thomas was absent in two sessions. It also includes some weakly grounded or invented claims, such as saying he 'treated the material with care' despite notes about card-handling reminders, and it risks attributing enjoyment of the 'Why are you so sad' story to a session where Thomas was absent, though another note partially supports phrase repetition. Behaviour is also arguably rated too positively given the mixed notes. Overall it is partially compliant but has notable grounding and absence-handling issues.

[{"role":"system","content":"You are grading output according to a user-specified rubric. If the statement in the rubric is true, then the output passes the test. You respond with a JSON object with this structure: {reason: string, pass: boolean, score: number} Examples: Hello world Content contains a greeting {"reason": "the content contains the word 'Hello'", "pass": true, "score": 1.0} Avast ye swabs, repel the invaders! Does not speak like a pirate {"reason": "'avast ye' is a common pirate term", "pass": false, "score": 0.0}"},{"role":"user","content":"

Kids&Us

Objectives and content of the Sam course, 3rd term

Term report (3rd term)

Data

Name: Thomas
Teacher: [Not present in notes]
Level: Sam Saturday
Absences: [Not present in notes]
Frequency of use (audio): [Not present in notes]

Participation/Motivation

Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Good

Comment:

Thomas was attentive and did what was asked of him on occasion. He showed understanding of vocabulary such as moods, genders, and weather, and could perform the gestures for the weather. However, he was not overly participative at times and needed encouragement to engage fully in the group routines. He enjoyed the activities, particularly the 'Why are you so sad' story, where he could repeat phrases well.

Learning process

Assessment (Learning process): Good

Comment:

Thomas demonstrated a solid grasp of vocabulary and structures. He could tell moods, genders, and weather, and could count very well. He paired numbers with spots effectively and repeated phrases accurately. However, he needed help to recall the full structure at times and required assistance to identify animals. His performance in the test was strong, though he did not use gestures like the others during the "Let's go to the zoo" activity.

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Good

Comment:

Thomas generally followed instructions and participated in activities. He treated the material with care but, at times, required reminders to follow group norms, such as not bending cards. He was cooperative in most tasks but showed some resistance in specific moments, such as refusing to high-five after repeated reminders about card handling.

Final comment

Thomas has shown steady progress in his learning, particularly in vocabulary recall and participation. While he sometimes needed encouragement to fully engage, his enthusiasm for activities like story time and counting games was evident. Continued practice with the audio tracks would help reinforce the vocabulary and structures learned this term.
